eval_results.name: state-block-generation
eval_results.key_finding: Generated well-formed UPMv2-STATE block with correct task statuses and machine-readable fencing

eval_results.name: task-status-update
eval_results.key_finding: T2 updated to completed; T1 and T3 preserved; percentage recalculated to 66.7%

eval_results.name: state-token-generation
eval_results.key_finding: Generated compact stateToken encoding 40% completion (2/5) with iteration context

eval_results.name: percentage-calculation
eval_results.key_finding: Correctly computed 60% with 0.5 partial credit for in_progress tasks (5 + 2*0.5 = 6/10)

eval_results.name: incremental-update
eval_results.key_finding: Incremental update: T3 set to completed, T6 added; existing state preserved; 50% recalculated

strengths: Strict UPMv2-STATE format compliance with machine-readable block fencing
Accurate percentage calculation including partial credit for in_progress tasks
Safe incremental updates — no destructive overwrites of existing task state
Compact stateToken generation encoding project/iteration/progress metadata
Clear task status transition tracking (pending → in_progress → completed)
weaknesses: stateToken format not yet standardized — encoding scheme may vary
No conflict detection when concurrent updates modify the same task
recommendations: Standardize stateToken encoding format (e.g. base64 JSON or hex digest)
Add optimistic locking / version field to state block for concurrent update safety
Test with large task lists (50+ tasks) to validate performance
